content:
Member-only story

5 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Image generated by GeminiOver the past year, I’ve been delegating more and more tasks to AI agents. And in April, when Claude Cowork finally made its debut in Germany, I doubled down. I built an agent to analyze my health stats and write daily reports on how to increase my performance. Another one conducts a weekly scan of the latest AI research and prepares a synopsis of the papers that I would be most interested in based on what I’ve written about. The one that has saved me the most time and brain power did my Belegprüfung (document audit) for me. I’d love to explain to you what that is, but I barely understand it myself. Which is why I was quite happy to delegate the task. Even if it performed poorly, AI would do a better job than I ever could.
As I assigned more tasks to AI agents, I noticed myself becoming less scrupulous about fact-checking the results. Two years ago, I would never have taken content generated by LLMs at face value. But now I find myself regularly skipping my meticulous inspections and trusting LLMs to get it right.
LLMs have gotten significantly better. They hallucinate less. They are less obviously sycophantic. Their built-in chain-of-thought “thinking” processes help them self-correct. But that’s not the only reason I and many of you have become more willing to trust AI responses.
